forecast.name: Tonight
forecast.detailedForecast: Snow before 11pm. Partly cloudy, with a low around 29. Northwest wind around 9 mph. Chance of precipitation is 80%. New snow accumulation of less than half an inch possible.

forecast.name: Friday
forecast.detailedForecast: Mostly sunny, with a high near 43. West wind 6 to 10 mph, with gusts as high as 21 mph.

weather_report:
As the evening descends upon Boston, a gentle layer of snow begins to cloak the city, adding a hushed beauty to the streets. The temperature will dip to a brisk 29 degrees, inviting a coziness that feels fitting for such a snowy night. With the northwest wind softly stirring at around 9 mph, it may feel just a touch nippy, underscoring the charm of winter evenings. The forecast hints at the possibility of light snow accumulations, making the world outside a scene pulled from a quiet storybook.

Looking ahead to Friday, the day promises a clear transformation. The clouds are expected to part, revealing a mostly sunny sky with a pleasant high near 43 degrees, accompanied by a gentle westward breeze. As the icy grip of winter loosens, the blossoming sunlight will beckon residents outside. A warm jacket paired with a scarf would serve you well, but expect to shed a layer as the sun brightens the day. Given the evolving weather, a stroll along the Charles or a leisurely visit to one of Boston's many quaint cafés could be the perfect way to savor this transitional season, encountering the city anew as it wakes from its frosty slumber.
